title: Drederick findings
description: Scope-enforced recon findings emitted by the drederick harness. See docs/DATASETTE.md for the schema reference.

findings.description: SQLite database written by Drederick.Reporting.SqliteReport at the end of every run (out/findings.db). One row per discovered host, service, finding, CVE, PoC reference, fetched PoC artifact, and detected operator tool.

hosts.title: Hosts
hosts.description: One row per in-scope target that produced at least one finding. Keyed by address (IPv4/IPv6 string); hostname is populated from the DNS probe when available.

services.title: Services
services.description: TCP/UDP services discovered by the nmap probe, one row per (host, port, proto). product/version come from nmap -sV.

findings.title: Findings
findings.description: Raw probe output keyed to a host (and optionally a service). 'kind' is the probe type (nmap, nmap-script, http, tls, dns, cve, ...). 'data_json' is the structured payload for that finding.

cves.title: CVEs
cves.description: CVE records annotated onto services by the enrichment layer. 'cvss' is the numeric base score (0.0-10.0); 'published' is an ISO-8601 timestamp. Facet buckets are shown raw — for score bands or year buckets, use the canned queries.

poc_refs.title: PoC references
poc_refs.description: Pointers to public proof-of-concept material for a given CVE (ExploitDB ID, GitHub repo, Metasploit module name, etc). References only — Drederick never executes PoCs.

poc_sources.title: PoC sources (fetched artifacts)
poc_sources.description: Local cache of fetched PoC content keyed by (source, external_id) with sha256 for integrity. Used to de-duplicate downloads across runs.

tooling.title: Operator tooling
tooling.description: Result of `drederick doctor` — detected operator-workstation tools (nmap, searchsploit, datasette, ...), their versions, and where they were found on PATH.

empire_servers.title: Empire servers
empire_servers.description: Empire C2 teamserver instances launched by Drederick. One row per (host, port, started_at). 'status' is 'running' while the server is alive and transitions to 'stopped' when shut down.

empire_listeners.title: Empire listeners
empire_listeners.description: Listeners registered on an Empire server (http, http_hop, dbx, smb, ...). Unique on (server_id, external_id).

empire_agents.title: Empire agents
empire_agents.description: Empire implants that have checked in. Joined to the originating teamserver, listener, and (when resolvable) the recon host row.

host_id.description: Foreign key → hosts.id. Links the agent back to the recon host row.

empire_modules_executed.title: Empire modules executed
empire_modules_executed.description: Per-invocation record of every Empire module dispatched against an agent. argv_digest and output_digest are SHA-256 hashes — plaintext output stays in out/<host>/empire/.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Researcher annotations: flagged findings, tactical notes, file attachments, and categorized observations linked to hosts or services.

is_flag.description: True if this note is marked as a tactical flag (📍).

flag_format.description: Flag style or format when is_flag=true.

tags.description: Comma-separated tags for filtering and search.

host_id.description: Foreign key → hosts.id. Links note to a target host.

service_id.description: Foreign key → services.id. Links note to a specific service.

file_size.description: Size in bytes of the attachment.

is_archived.description: True if the note is archived (soft-deleted).

cves_by_host.title: CVEs by host
cves_by_host.description: Every (host, service) that has at least one CVE finding, joined to the cves table. Ordered by CVSS descending.
cves_by_host.sql: SELECT h.address, s.port, s.service, s.product, s.version, c.cve_id, c.cvss, c.summary FROM hosts h JOIN services s ON s.host_id = h.id JOIN findings f ON f.service_id = s.id JOIN cves c ON c.cve_id = json_extract(f.data_json, '$.cve_id') WHERE f.kind = 'cve' ORDER BY c.cvss DESC

services_with_pocs.title: Services with public PoCs
services_with_pocs.description: Services whose annotated CVE has at least one entry in poc_refs. Ordered by CVSS descending, then host/port for stability.
services_with_pocs.sql: SELECT h.address, s.port, s.service, s.product, s.version, c.cve_id, c.cvss, p.source AS poc_source, p.external_id, p.url FROM hosts h JOIN services s ON s.host_id = h.id JOIN findings f ON f.service_id = s.id JOIN cves c ON c.cve_id = json_extract(f.data_json, '$.cve_id') JOIN poc_refs p ON p.cve_id = c.cve_id WHERE f.kind = 'cve' ORDER BY c.cvss DESC, h.address, s.port

pocs_by_source.title: PoC references grouped by source
pocs_by_source.description: Count of PoC references per upstream source (exploit-db, github, metasploit, ...).
pocs_by_source.sql: SELECT source, COUNT(*) AS ref_count FROM poc_refs GROUP BY source ORDER BY ref_count DESC

tooling_detected.title: Operator tooling detected
tooling_detected.description: All tools detected by `drederick doctor` in alphabetical order.
tooling_detected.sql: SELECT * FROM tooling ORDER BY name

top_cves_by_cvss.title: Top 50 CVEs by CVSS
top_cves_by_cvss.description: Top 50 CVEs annotated onto this run, highest CVSS first. NULL CVSS values sort last.
top_cves_by_cvss.sql: SELECT cve_id, cvss, summary FROM cves ORDER BY cvss DESC NULLS LAST LIMIT 50

empire_active_agents.title: Empire — active agents
empire_active_agents.description: All Empire agents currently reporting status='active', joined to their teamserver, listener, and recon host row. Sorted by most-recent check-in.
empire_active_agents.sql: SELECT a.external_id AS agent, a.user_name, a.process_name, a.pid, a.os, a.language, h.address AS host, l.name AS listener, l.type AS listener_type, s.host AS server_host, s.port AS server_port, a.checkin_at, a.last_seen_at FROM empire_agents a JOIN empire_servers s ON s.id = a.server_id LEFT JOIN empire_listeners l ON l.id = a.listener_id LEFT JOIN hosts h ON h.id = a.host_id WHERE a.status = 'active' ORDER BY a.last_seen_at DESC NULLS LAST, a.checkin_at DESC

empire_agents_per_host.title: Empire — agents per host
empire_agents_per_host.description: Count of Empire agents per recon host, including offline ones. Useful for spotting hosts with multiple implants or stale agents to clean up.
empire_agents_per_host.sql: SELECT h.address, COUNT(a.id) AS total_agents, SUM(CASE WHEN a.status = 'active' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS active_agents, SUM(CASE WHEN a.status != 'active' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS offline_agents, MAX(a.last_seen_at) AS last_seen FROM hosts h JOIN empire_agents a ON a.host_id = h.id GROUP BY h.id, h.address ORDER BY active_agents DESC, total_agents DESC, h.address

empire_modules_recent.title: Empire — recent module executions
empire_modules_recent.description: Last 50 Empire module invocations across every agent, joined to the agent and host. Plaintext output isn't stored — only argv_digest / output_digest SHA-256 hashes.
empire_modules_recent.sql: SELECT m.executed_at, m.module_name, m.exit_status, a.external_id AS agent, a.user_name AS agent_user, h.address AS host, m.argv_digest, m.output_digest FROM empire_modules_executed m JOIN empire_agents a ON a.id = m.agent_id LEFT JOIN hosts h ON h.id = a.host_id ORDER BY m.executed_at DESC LIMIT 50

empire_creds_harvested.title: Empire — credentials harvested
empire_creds_harvested.description: Loot captured by Empire (realm = 'empire-loot' in loot.metadata), joined to the agent on the same host. value_sha256 is the only representation of the secret — plaintext stays in out/<host>/loot/.
empire_creds_harvested.sql: SELECT l.captured_at, l.target AS host, l.kind, l.value_sha256, l.source_tool, json_extract(l.metadata, '$.username') AS captured_user, json_extract(l.metadata, '$.domain') AS captured_domain, a.external_id AS agent, a.user_name AS agent_user FROM loot l LEFT JOIN hosts h ON h.address = l.target LEFT JOIN empire_agents a ON a.host_id = h.id WHERE json_extract(l.metadata, '$.realm') = 'empire-loot' ORDER BY l.captured_at DESC

empire_listener_summary.title: Empire — listener inventory
empire_listener_summary.description: Every Empire listener with its parent teamserver and a per-listener agent count (total / active).
empire_listener_summary.sql: SELECT l.name, l.type, l.listen_host, l.listen_port, s.host AS server_host, s.port AS server_port, s.status AS server_status, COUNT(a.id) AS agent_count, SUM(CASE WHEN a.status = 'active' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS active_agents, l.created_at FROM empire_listeners l JOIN empire_servers s ON s.id = l.server_id LEFT JOIN empire_agents a ON a.listener_id = l.id GROUP BY l.id, l.name, l.type, l.listen_host, l.listen_port, s.host, s.port, s.status, l.created_at ORDER BY l.created_at DESC

empire_lifecycle_audit.title: Empire — server lifecycle timeline
empire_lifecycle_audit.description: Start/stop timeline of every Empire teamserver instance Drederick has tracked. 'lifecycle' is 'running' while stopped_at is NULL.
empire_lifecycle_audit.sql: SELECT s.id, s.host, s.port, s.pid, s.started_at, s.stopped_at, s.status, CASE WHEN s.stopped_at IS NULL THEN 'running' ELSE 'stopped' END AS lifecycle FROM empire_servers s ORDER BY s.started_at DESC

empire_host_compromise_chain.title: Empire — host compromise chain
empire_host_compromise_chain.description: Per-host overview from initial recon → exploit_runs → empire_agents → modules_executed → harvested empire-loot credentials. Hosts with zero exploit / Empire activity are filtered out. Use as the landing query for post-exploitation triage.
empire_host_compromise_chain.sql: WITH host_summary AS (SELECT h.id AS host_id, h.address, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM findings f WHERE f.host_id = h.id) AS findings_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM exploit_runs er WHERE er.target = h.address) AS exploit_runs, (SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN er.exit_code = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM exploit_runs er WHERE er.target = h.address) AS exploit_successes, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM empire_agents a WHERE a.host_id = h.id) AS empire_agents, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM empire_agents a WHERE a.host_id = h.id AND a.status = 'active') AS empire_active, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM empire_modules_executed m JOIN empire_agents a ON a.id = m.agent_id WHERE a.host_id = h.id) AS modules_executed, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM loot l WHERE l.target = h.address AND json_extract(l.metadata, '$.realm') = 'empire-loot') AS empire_creds FROM hosts h) SELECT * FROM host_summary WHERE exploit_runs > 0 OR empire_agents > 0 ORDER BY empire_creds DESC, modules_executed DESC, empire_active DESC, exploit_successes DESC, address
